LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.CleaningTool.WrongFilthCa: Tool not suited to this filth
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.CleaningTool.SpecialtyExp: The cleaning speed bonus from this tool only applies to dry filth. Wet filth is cleaned at regular speed.
The cleaning speed bonus from this tool only applies to wet filth. Dry filth is cleaned at regular speed.
The cleaning speed bonus from this tool only applies while clearing polluted terrain. Ordinary filth is cleaned at regular speed.
This tool dematerialises the entire filth stack on the target tile in a single pass, not just the topmost piece.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.CleaningPref.Desc.Any: This tool's bearer will pick up cleaning jobs regardless of whether the filth is indoors or outdoors. Click to restrict to indoors only.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.CleaningPref.Desc.Indoors: This tool's bearer will ignore filth outdoors, only cleaning rooms enclosed by walls and roof. Click to restrict to outdoors only.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.CleaningPref.Desc.Outdoor: This tool's bearer will ignore indoor filth, only cleaning outside or in unenclosed spaces. Click to clear the restriction.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.CleaningArea.Desc.None: This tool's bearer will pick up cleaning jobs anywhere on the map. Click to restrict cleaning to a player-painted area.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.CleaningArea.Desc.Restric: This tool's bearer will only clean filth inside the "{0}" area. If that area is renamed or deleted, the restriction is dropped. Click to choose a different area or clear the restriction.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.Thought.FreshlyCleanedRoo: That room was spotless. I'm glad we have a janitor around.
LanguageData.JanitorsCloset.Settings.DebugLoggingTool: Log per-tick decisions about cleaning-tool sound swaps and CleaningSpeed/GeneralLaborSpeed adjustments. Verbose — turn on only when investigating an issue.
